mechanism_narrative: A lower effective federal funds rate relative to its trailing 12-month average reduces the risk-free discount rate, causing equity valuations to re-rate upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS is below its 12-month average are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness triggers wage acceleration fears and leads the Federal Reserve to tighten policy faster than priced, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE is below 4.5%, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and residential housing starts indicates broadening domestic demand momentum that outpaces supply, driving corporate revenue surprises and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of RSAFS and HOUST both rising fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) over individual series in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month increase in consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes beyond market expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day return frequency exceeds 55% during tightening cycle months with rising CPIAUCSL, or the rank correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and forward returns is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger monthly readings in retail sales directly translate into higher expected corporate revenues for consumer-facing sectors, pulling aggregate S&P 500 earnings expectations up and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes show Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day return frequency is below 52% in the top quintile of RSAFS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines by more than 1 percent over a rolling three-month window, it signals contraction in goods-producing sectors that drags aggregate earnings growth lower, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency falls below 55% when INDPRO 3-month change is below -1.0%, or the signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative mean return (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in payroll employment and a simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate indicate a labor market demand surge that intensifies wage-push inflation fears, leading to tighter monetary policy expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE changes fails to add significant explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression, or the joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 1.0 percent that rises month-over-month signals ongoing monetary normalization; further rate increases reduce the present value of future cash flows and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day return frequency exceeds 55% when FEDFUNDS is above 1.0% and rising, or the rank correlation between month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes and 5-day forward returns is positive over a 2-year window.
